task_completion.description: Create a map plot for Sea Level Pressure (SLP) for the month of July using NASA Panoply.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A Panoply map plot window is open showing 'Sea Level Pressure'. The time/date dimension (usually visible in the Array tab or plot subtitle) must be set to July.

task_completion.description: Create a map plot for Precipitation Rate for the month of July using NASA Panoply.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A Panoply map plot window is open showing 'Precipitation Rate'. The time/date dimension must be set to July.

task_completion.description: Export the SLP map to the specified directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save As' dialog in Panoply or a file manager window showing the file 'slp_global_july.png' being saved to or existing in the '~/Documents/DesertBelt' directory.

task_completion.description: Export the precipitation map to the specified directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save As' dialog in Panoply or a file manager window showing the file 'precip_global_july.png' being saved to or existing in the '~/Documents/DesertBelt' directory.

task_completion.description: Draft the assessment report detailing the relationship between SLP and precipitation.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor (e.g., gedit, nano) is open showing the file 'desert_belt_report.txt'. The text should explicitly mention the NEGATIVE relationship between high sea level pressure and precipitation (e.g., high SLP corresponds to low precipitation).

integrity.description: The agent must use the NASA Panoply GUI to generate the plots.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the actual Panoply Java application interface being interacted with, rather than terminal commands running Python scripts to generate the images.

integrity.description: The agent must actually select the month of July for the plots.
integrity.visual_evidence: In the Panoply plot window, the 'Array' or 'Time' settings tab must show July selected. The agent should not just export the default first time step (January) and deceptively name the file with 'july'.

integrity.description: The agent should draft the report based on its analysis.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent should be seen writing or editing the report in a text editor, rather than using a single terminal command (like echo) to blindly write a pre-computed answer without analyzing the plots.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Verified facts for the VLM verifier: The expected relationship between Sea Level Pressure (SLP) and Precipitation Rate is NEGATIVE (high SLP regions like the Azores High have significantly lower precipitation than low SLP regions like the ITCZ). The analysis must be for the month of July. Expected output files are 'slp_global_july.png', 'precip_global_july.png', and 'desert_belt_report.txt' located in '/home/ga/Documents/DesertBelt'.
